name: FINOS Architecture As Code
description: Complete CALM architecture of the FINOS architecture-as-code repository: CALM Hub registry, CALM Hub UI, CALM CLI, CALM Server, CALMGuard compliance platform, CalmStudio desktop, CalmStudio MCP Server, CALM VSCode Extension, and supporting infrastructure.

nodes.name: Developer / Architect
nodes.description: Human developer or architect who authors, validates, and explores CALM architecture models using the toolchain.

nodes.name: AI Assistant
nodes.description: AI coding assistant or MCP client (e.g. Claude, Copilot) that drives CALM operations through MCP tooling.

nodes.name: CALM Hub
nodes.description: Java/Quarkus REST API serving as the central registry for CALM architectures, patterns, flows, standards, interfaces, controls, ADRs, and decorators; includes an embedded MCP server. Exposes HTTP on port 8080 and HTTPS on port 8443 (secure profile).

interfaces.description: HTTP REST API for CALM Hub resources including namespaces, architectures, patterns, flows, standards, interfaces, controls, ADRs, and decorators.
HTTPS REST API for CALM Hub (secure profile) with JWT authentication enforced on all /calm/* paths.
Embedded Model Context Protocol (MCP) server exposing CALM Hub operations as JSON-RPC tools to AI assistants.

nodes.name: CALM Hub UI
nodes.description: React/Vite single-page application for browsing, searching, and visualising CALM resources stored in CALM Hub. Proxies /calm/* to CALM Hub on port 8080; uses oidc-client-ts for OIDC authentication when the secure profile is active.

interfaces.description: React SPA served by Vite development server; in production served as static assets bundled within the CALM Hub Quarkus container.

nodes.name: MongoDB
nodes.description: MongoDB document store used by CALM Hub to persist all CALM resources via the Java MongoDB driver. Database name: calmSchemas.

interfaces.description: MongoDB wire protocol connection on port 27017; CALM Hub connects using database name 'calmSchemas'.

nodes.name: CALM Server
nodes.description: Standalone TypeScript/Express HTTP server exposing a CALM document validation endpoint and health check. Binds to 127.0.0.1:3000 by default; bundles CALM meta-schemas; no authentication or authorisation controls.

interfaces.description: Accepts CALM architecture JSON and validates it against bundled CALM meta-schemas and Spectral linting rules; returns validation results including errors and warnings.
Health check endpoint returning service status.

nodes.name: CALM CLI
nodes.description: Node.js command-line executable (calm) providing generate, validate, docify, template, and init-ai commands. Optionally connects to a CALM Hub instance for remote schema resolution via --calm-hub-url.

nodes.name: CALMGuard
nodes.description: Next.js 15 full-stack AI-powered CALM compliance analysis application deployed to Vercel. Uses SSE streaming for analysis results; calls LLM providers via the Vercel AI SDK; integrates with GitHub API.

interfaces.description: SSE streaming endpoint that runs 4 AI agents (architecture analyser, compliance mapper, risk scorer, calm remediator) over a CALM architecture and streams AgentEvents in real-time.
Validates a CALM JSON document by spawning a calm-cli subprocess; returns validation results including errors.
Parses a CALM document JSON payload and returns a structured representation for further processing.
Fetches a CALM architecture file from a specified GitHub repository owner, repo, and file path.
Creates a GitHub pull request with AI-generated CALM architecture content or remediation suggestions.
Pipeline execution endpoint for triggering multi-step CALM analysis and generation workflows.

nodes.name: CalmStudio Desktop
nodes.description: Tauri desktop application for visually creating and editing CALM architecture files; wraps a SvelteKit UI in a native cross-platform desktop container.

nodes.name: CalmStudio MCP Server
nodes.description: Node.js MCP server exposing CALM authoring tools (create_architecture, add_node, add_relationship, validate, render, etc.) to AI assistants via stdio (default) or HTTP on port 3100.

interfaces.description: Default stdio transport for MCP clients such as Claude Code; exposes tools for creating and managing CALM architecture documents.
Optional HTTP transport for the CalmStudio MCP server, activated with --http and optionally --port flags.

nodes.name: CALM VSCode Extension
nodes.description: VS Code extension providing CALM model editing, Mermaid diagram preview, tree-view navigation, inline schema validation diagnostics, and docify site generation. Uses @finos/calm-shared in-process for validation (no HTTP call to calm-server).

nodes.name: Keycloak Identity Provider
nodes.description: Keycloak OIDC identity provider used for authentication and authorisation in the secure profile. CALM Hub validates JWT bearer tokens against it; CALM Hub UI performs the OIDC authorisation-code flow. Runs on HTTPS port 9443 in local dev.

interfaces.description: Keycloak OIDC realm endpoint providing discovery, authorisation, token issuance, JWKS, and logout endpoints for the calm-hub-realm.

nodes.name: GitHub Actions
nodes.description: GitHub Actions CI/CD platform that builds, tests, and publishes all CALM toolchain components; publishes the CALM Hub Docker image to Docker Hub and syncs documentation to AWS S3.

nodes.name: Docker Hub
nodes.description: Docker Hub container registry where the finos/calm-hub multi-arch Docker image is published on release by GitHub Actions.

nodes.name: AWS S3
nodes.description: AWS S3 buckets used to host the built Docusaurus documentation site and video assets, synced via GitHub Actions workflows.

nodes.name: LLM Providers
nodes.description: External large-language-model API providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, xAI) called by CALMGuard's AI agent orchestrator via the Vercel AI SDK.

nodes.name: GitHub REST API
nodes.description: The GitHub REST API (api.github.com) called by CALMGuard to fetch CALM files from repositories and to create pull requests.

interfaces.description: GitHub REST API v3 endpoints for repository content access (/repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents) and pull request creation (/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls).

relationships.description: Browses, searches, and visualises CALM resources through the web interface.
Runs generate, validate, docify, template, and init-ai commands from the terminal.
Posts CALM architecture JSON to the standalone validation endpoint.
Uploads or fetches CALM models and reviews AI-generated compliance analysis results.
Authors and explores CALM models visually using the desktop application.
Edits and validates CALM files within VS Code with real-time diagnostics and diagram preview.
Invokes CALM Hub MCP tools (list/create architectures, decorators, namespaces, search) via the embedded MCP server at /mcp.
Uses CalmStudio MCP tools to create, edit, and validate CALM architecture files.
Sends Axios HTTP requests to /calm/namespaces/* REST endpoints to fetch and display architectures, patterns, flows, controls, decorators, and search results.
Performs the OAuth2 authorisation-code flow and silent token renewal via oidc-client-ts when the secure profile is enabled.
Retrieves remote CALM schemas and documents from the CALM Hub REST API when a --calm-hub-url is supplied.
Persists and retrieves all CALM resources using the MongoDB Java driver over the MongoDB wire protocol on port 27017.
Validates JWT bearer tokens on /calm/* and /mcp/* routes by introspecting against the Keycloak JWKS endpoint via the Quarkus OIDC extension (secure profile only).
Spawns the calm-cli Node.js subprocess to validate CALM documents server-side for the /api/calm/validate endpoint.
Calls external LLM provider APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, xAI) via the Vercel AI SDK to run the AI compliance-analysis agent pipeline.
Calls the GitHub REST API to fetch CALM files from repositories and to create pull requests with generated architecture or remediation content.
Reads and writes CALM documents via the CALM Hub REST API from the desktop application.
Builds and pushes the finos/calm-hub multi-arch Docker image to Docker Hub on release via the docker-publish-calm-hub.yml workflow.
Uploads the built Docusaurus documentation site and video assets to S3 via s3-docs-sync.yml and s3-video-sync.yml workflows.
